summary: Re-verified get_current_user fix and all newly added validations. Backend pytest suite (24 tests) and full frontend E2E flow both pass against the live preview URL. No critical issues found.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~700 lines and mixes auth/games/tournaments/chat/wallet routers in one file. Recommend splitting into routers/ for maintainability.
Tournament 'complete' endpoint uses winner_user_id as a query param (POST). For RESTful clarity prefer a JSON body, e.g. {'winner_user_id': '...'}.

Wallet balance can only be credited (a) via Stripe webhook or (b) by completing a tournament — no admin endpoint exists. Tests had to seed wallet directly via pymongo. Consider an admin endpoint or test-only seed flag.
CreateTournament page registers route /create-tournament but a common URL pattern would be /tournaments/create. Document the route convention; minor inconsistency only.
Chat polling/refresh not observed — chat appears to only refresh on send. Confirm whether real-time updates (websocket/polling) are required by PRD.
test_credentials: Admin admin@esportsbet.com / admin123 works (cookie returned). Generated transient TEST_ users per run; wallet seeded via direct Mongo update for staking flow.
seed_data_creation: TEST_ prefixed users, games, and tournaments are created per test run. Pymongo used to credit wallet balances (no admin credit endpoint exists).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py covers: (1) auth register/login/me + get_current_user user['id'] fix, (2) stake>0 validation (rejects 0 and negative), (3) winner-must-be-participant validation, (4) chat restricted to participants (403 for non-participant, 200 for participants), (5) creator-only completion (403 otherwise), (6) prize payout = stake*players*0.95 credited to winner, (7) already-completed tournament rejection, (8) Stripe deposit returns checkout_url+session_id and status endpoint works. Tests require MongoDB localhost:27017 with DB 'test_database' for wallet seeding. Frontend E2E verified via Playwright on the preview URL: register/login -> dashboard -> create game -> create tournament (with stake) -> redirect to /tournament/:id -> post chat as participant -> stake=0 client-side block -> wallet -> deposit -> redirected to checkout.stripe.com. Note frontend routes are /create-tournament and /tournament/:id (NOT /tournaments/create). A harmless console warning about <span> inside <option> originates from the Emergent visual editor instrumentation, not app code.
rca of the issue: Previous iteration's 500 errors were caused by get_current_user not exposing user['id'] while all protected handlers consumed it. Fix at server.py line ~68 (user['id'] = str(user['_id'])) is confirmed working: GET /api/auth/me returns 200 with id, and every downstream protected endpoint (games, tournaments, chat, wallet) works end-to-end. Newly added validations confirmed: POST /api/tournaments rejects stake_amount<=0 with 400; POST /api/tournaments/{id}/complete rejects winner_user_id not in participants with 400 and non-creator caller with 403; POST /api/chat rejects non-participants with 403.
